plan.task: summarize conversation history to reduce memory usage while preserving all critical data
execute Python code to calculate/analyze data based on user request
plan.verify: are the calculations accurate and properly formatted

plan.task: analyze uploaded files (images, PDFs, documents) if present
synthesize results from previous steps into final user response

required_fields.goal: string - Brief restatement of user's objective

plan_step_schema.id: string (required) - Unique step identifier (s1, s2, etc.)
plan_step_schema.agent: string (required) - Name of agent to execute this step
plan_step_schema.depends_on: array (optional) - Array of step IDs this step depends on. Default: []
plan_step_schema.verify: string|null (optional) - Verification question for LLM validation. Use null to skip verification
plan_step_schema.timeout_seconds: integer (optional) - Max execution time. Default: 120 seconds
plan_step_schema.retry: integer (optional) - Number of retry attempts on failure. Default: 0

available_agents.conversation_summarizer: Intelligently summarize conversation history to reduce memory usage while preserving all critical data. Use when word_count > 2000
available_agents.python_exec_agent: Execute Python code with guaranteed tool calling support. For math, computation, data analysis tasks
available_agents.file_analyzer_agent: Analyze various file types including images, PDFs, and documents. Use when files are uploaded
available_agents.human_response_agent: Final response formatting and synthesis. Always use as final step for user-facing output

dynamic_summarization_logic.word_count > 1500: CONSIDER summarization if response will be complex

conversation_context_priority.note: If user input contains 'Previous conversation context (words: N):' with [Turn-X] markers
actions: PRIORITIZE existing data in planning - use as foundation instead of generating new data
BUILD UPON previous interactions to maintain continuity
EXTRACT specific data using [Turn-X] markers when available
NOTE: Large word counts indicate rich conversation history with preserved structured data

parallel_execution.note: Steps with no dependencies can run in parallel
parallel_execution.example: Steps s2 and s3 above can run simultaneously since s2 depends on s1 but s3 has no dependencies

verification_best_practices.format: Simple yes/no questions that can be answered by LLM
examples: are the calculations accurate and properly formatted
verification_best_practices.when_to_skip: Use null for final steps (human_response_agent) or when verification adds no value
